description_long: This object is a vintage computer monitor with a beige plastic exterior, featuring a prominent CRT display framed by a thick bezel. The front view reveals a large, slightly curved screen displaying yellow text on a black background, with a built-in floppy disk drive and a keyboard tray beneath. The left side showcases two large, circular ventilation fans set within a black panel, while the right side has a vented section and a red power button. The rear is mostly flat with a central vented panel and a protruding section at the top, likely for additional cooling. The monitor sits on a wide, rectangular base that provides stability and houses additional controls and drive slots. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function.
description: A beige, vintage CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, built-in floppy drive, and a wide rectangular base. It features prominent ventilation fans on one side and a utilitarian, functional design.
description_view_0: The rear view shows a flat beige surface with a central vented panel and a protruding section at the top, emphasizing the monitor's cooling system.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights two large, circular ventilation fans set within a black panel, along with the monitor's angular, geometric shape.
description_view_2: The front view displays the CRT screen with yellow text, a thick bezel, a built-in floppy drive, and a keyboard tray, making it the primary interaction side.
description_view_3: The right side view reveals a vented section, a red power button, and a slot, with a generally flat and unadorned surface.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rectangular, and made of beige plastic with a matte finish, featuring sharp corners and no decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a wide, rectangular flat base made of matching beige plastic, providing stability without visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: Physical buttons are located on the lower right of the front panel and the right side near the base, grouped together for easy access.
